vulnerability.hypothesis: In IsObjectEscaped (line 284), when a scalar-replaced object is used as the VALUE operand (operand index 1) of MPostWriteBarrier, the escape analysis unconditionally breaks (treating it as non-escaped). But MPostWriteBarrier has two operands: object (index 0) and value (index 1). If the scalar-replaced object appears as operand 1 (being stored INTO another object), it truly escapes — it's being written to a slot of another live object. The escape analysis fails to detect this, causing the object to be scalar-replaced even though a reference to it exists in another object's slot. After scalar replacement removes the allocation, the other object holds a dangling/invalid reference, leading to type confusion or use-after-free when the stored reference is later read. Compare with StoreFixedSlot (line 274-282) which correctly checks `def->indexOf(*i) == 0` and marks as escaped if the object is not operand 0.
